segments.text: And David numbered the people that were with him, and set captains of thousands and captains of hundreds over them.

And David sent forth a third part of the people under the hand of Joab, and a third part under the hand of Abishai the son of Zeruiah, Joab's brother, and a third part under the hand of Ittai the Gittite.
I will surely go forth with you myself also.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Thou shalt not go forth: for if we flee away, they will not care for us; neither if half of us die, will they care for us: but now thou art worth ten thousand of us: therefore now it is better that thou succour us out of the city.
segments.speaker: People

footnotes: care…: Heb. set their heart on us
worth…: Heb. as ten thousand of us
succour: Heb. be to succour or help

segments.text: What seemeth you best I will do.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: And the king stood by the gate side, and all the people came out by hundreds and by thousands.
Deal gently for my sake with the young man, even with Absalom.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: And all the people heard when the king gave all the captains charge concerning Absalom.
So the people went out into the field against Israel: and the battle was in the wood of Ephraim;

heading: Battle in the Wood of Ephraim

segments.text: Where the people of Israel were slain before the servants of David, and there was there a great slaughter that day of twenty thousand men.
For the battle was there scattered over the face of all the country: and the wood devoured more people that day than the sword devoured.

And Absalom met the servants of David. And Absalom rode upon a mule, and the mule went under the thick boughs of a great oak, and his head caught hold of the oak, and he was taken up between the heaven and the earth; and the mule that was under him went away.
And a certain man saw it, and told Joab,
Behold, I saw Absalom hanged in an oak.
segments.speaker: Unnamed Man

segments.text: And, behold, thou sawest him, and why didst thou not smite him there to the ground? and I would have given thee ten shekels of silver, and a girdle.
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: Though I should receive a thousand shekels of silver in mine hand, yet would I not put forth mine hand against the king's son: for in our hearing the king charged thee and Abishai and Ittai, saying, Beware that none touch the young man Absalom.
segments.speaker: Unnamed Man

footnotes: receive: Heb. weigh upon mine hand
Beware…: Heb. Beware whosoever ye be of, etc

segments.text: Otherwise I should have wrought falsehood against mine own life: for there is no matter hid from the king, and thou thyself wouldest have set thyself against me.
segments.speaker: Unnamed Man

segments.text: I may not tarry thus with thee.
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: And he took three darts in his hand, and thrust them through the heart of Absalom, while he was yet alive in the midst of the oak.
And ten young men that bare Joab's armour compassed about and smote Absalom, and slew him.
And Joab blew the trumpet, and the people returned from pursuing after Israel: for Joab held back the people.
And they took Absalom, and cast him into a great pit in the wood, and laid a very great heap of stones upon him: and all Israel fled every one to his tent.

Now Absalom in his lifetime had taken and reared up for himself a pillar, which is in the king's dale: for he said, I have no son to keep my name in remembrance: and he called the pillar after his own name: and it is called unto this day, Absalom's place.
Let me now run, and bear the king tidings, how that the LORD hath avenged him of his enemies.
segments.speaker: Ahimaaz

footnotes: avenged…: Heb. judged him from the hand, etc
heading: Messengers to David

segments.text: Thou shalt not bear tidings this day, but thou shalt bear tidings another day: but this day thou shalt bear no tidings, because the king's son is dead.
segments.speaker: Joab

footnotes: bear tidings this: Heb. be a man of tidings, etc

segments.text: Go tell the king what thou hast seen.
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: And Cushi bowed himself unto Joab, and ran.
But howsoever, let me, I pray thee, also run after Cushi.
segments.speaker: Ahimaaz

segments.text: Wherefore wilt thou run, my son, seeing that thou hast no tidings ready?
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: Let me run.
segments.speaker: Ahimaaz

segments.text: Run.
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: Then Ahimaaz ran by the way of the plain, and overran Cushi.
And David sat between the two gates: and the watchman went up to the roof over the gate unto the wall, and lifted up his eyes, and looked, and behold a man running alone.
And the watchman cried, and told the king.
If he be alone, there is tidings in his mouth.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: And he came apace, and drew near.
And the watchman saw another man running: and the watchman called unto the porter, and said, Behold another man running alone.
He also bringeth tidings.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: And the watchman said,
Me thinketh the running of the foremost is like the running of Ahimaaz the son of Zadok.
segments.speaker: Watchman

segments.text: He is a good man, and cometh with good tidings.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: Me…: Heb. I see the running

segments.text: All is well.
segments.speaker: Ahimaaz

segments.text: And he fell down to the earth upon his face before the king,
Blessed be the LORD thy God, which hath delivered up the men that lifted up their hand against my lord the king.
segments.speaker: Ahimaaz

footnotes: All…: or, Peace be to thee: Heb. Peace

segments.text: Is the young man Absalom safe?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: When Joab sent the king's servant, and me thy servant, I saw a great tumult, but I knew not what it was.
segments.speaker: Ahimaaz

segments.text: Turn aside, and stand here.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: And he turned aside, and stood still.
Tidings, my lord the king: for the LORD hath avenged thee this day of all them that rose up against thee.
segments.speaker: Cushi

segments.text: Is the young man Absalom safe?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: The enemies of my lord the king, and all that rise against thee to do thee hurt, be as that young man is.
segments.speaker: Cushi

segments.text: And the king was much moved, and went up to the chamber over the gate, and wept:
O my son Absalom, my son, my son Absalom! would God I had died for thee, O Absalom, my son, my son!
segments.speaker: David
